title: ACT etag value
description: Per PRD-103-R1, PRD-103-R2, PRD-103-R3. Shape of the value carried in any envelope's `etag` field. General form is `<algorithm>:<hash>` where `<algorithm>` is a lowercase ASCII identifier and `<hash>` is base64url-safe (RFC 4648 §5) without padding. v0.1 admits exactly one algorithm: `s256` (SHA-256, base64url, no padding, truncated to 22 chars) — see `$defs.v0_1_algorithm_admit_list`. Adding a new algorithm value to the open admit-list is MINOR per PRD-108-R4(3); changing or removing `s256` is MAJOR per PRD-108-R5. The top-level `pattern` is intentionally loose so future MINOR additions remain valid against archived envelopes.

v0_1_algorithm_admit_list.description: v0.1 strict form (PRD-103-R3): algorithm MUST be `s256`, hash MUST be exactly 22 base64url-safe characters. The top-level schema enforces this strict form. A future MINOR per PRD-108-R4(3) MAY add additional algorithm prefixes; consumers reading future-MINOR envelopes MUST treat unknown algorithm prefixes as opaque revalidation tokens (PRD-103 forward-compat rule).

loose_future_minor_form.description: Loose forward-compat pattern admitting any lowercase ASCII algorithm prefix and base64url-safe hash. Provided for non-validating tooling that needs to recognize future-MINOR etag shapes; PRD-600 v0.1 enforces the strict form at top level.
